mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts indicates a supply-demand imbalance where manufacturing output outpaces residential construction, reflecting robust business investment that drives positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance in out-of-sample testing, or the signal predicts negative returns during confirmed supply booms.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, real interest rates turn positive and tighten financial conditions, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when real rates are deeply positive, or the forward return differential between tight and loose regimes is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures drives corporate revenue expansion, which directly supports earnings per share growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns during periods of actual earnings contraction, or the forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward positive outcomes when the series expands.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in new housing starts reflects weakening consumer confidence and tighter credit conditions, which precede broader economic slowdowns and cause negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly period.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1900.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Housing declines fail to precede equity drawdowns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates a self-reinforcing labor-income consumption loop that accelerates corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing, or positive returns do not materialize when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its long-term trend while unemployment remains below 5.5% signals an overheating economy where capacity constraints drive input cost inflation, ultimately compressing equity margins and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite confirmed capacity constraints and low unemployment, or the mechanism fails to predict margin compression during historical supply bottlenecks.
